perturbation.original_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure, and any information disclosed in non- tangible form that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and summ arized in a writing labelled as “confidential” delivered to Recipient within 15 days after disclosure. CI does not include information that (A) is or becomes generally publicly available at the time of disclosure or subsequently through no fault of Recipie nt, (B) was known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, before its disclosure by Discloser, (C) becomes known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, from a source other than Discloser, or (D) is independently developed by R ecipient without use of CI.
perturbation.changed_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. CI does not include information that (A) is or becomes generally publicly available at the time of disclosure or subsequently through no fault of Recipie nt, (B) was known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, before its disclosure by Discloser, (C) becomes known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, from a source other than Discloser, or (D) is independently developed by R ecipient without use of CI. CI also includes information, regardless of prior designations or markings, that Adobe, in its sole discretion, deems confidential at any time during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of CI focuses on information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. The added clause gives Adobe the right to retroactively designate any information as confidential, overriding the original constraints. This creates a conflict because the original definition excludes certain types of information from being considered CI, but the added clause allows Adobe to bypass those exclusions at any time, contradicting the initial definition. Specifically, it creates a contradiction in that information previously considered non-confidential under (A),(B),(C) and (D) may now be considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition . CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. CI does not include information that (A) is or becomes generally publicly available at the time of disclosure or subsequently through no fault of Recipie nt, (B) was known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, before its disclosure by Discloser, (C) becomes known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, from a source other than Discloser, or (D) is independently developed by R ecipient without use of CI. CI also includes information, regardless of prior designations or markings, that Adobe, in its sole discretion, deems confidential at any time during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause granting Adobe the discretion to deem any information confidential at any time, regardless of prior designations. This directly contradicts the original definition of CI, which excludes information that becomes publicly available, was already known to the recipient, or was independently developed. The added clause allows Adobe to retroactively classify previously excluded information as CI, creating a clear contradiction, especially because the contradicted text is identical to the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that representatives must be bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before accessing CI. The modified text omits this requirement. This creates a contradiction because now representatives can access CI without being bound by confidentiality agreements, which goes against the core purpose of an NDA. There is an in-text contradiction in the same section: initially representatives must be bound by confidentiality before accessing CI and now they don't have to be.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires representatives to be bound by confidentiality obligations before accessing CI, which aligns with the Contradicted Text emphasizing confidentiality obligations. The Changed Text removes this requirement, creating a direct contradiction. Now, representatives can access CI without confidentiality agreements, directly conflicting with the purpose of the NDA and the stipulations in the Contradicted Text and the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will treat all CI with the degree of care that Recipient wants.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a minimum standard of care for CI: at least the same as its own sensitive information, but never less than reasonable. The modified text gives the Recipient complete discretion. This creates a conflict because the original ensures a baseline protection, while the altered text could allow for negligent handling of CI if the recipient doesn't value the CI, because 'the degree of care that Recipient wants' could be interpreted as no care at all, contradicting the intent of a non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with the degree of care that Recipient wants. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a minimum standard of care for CI (at least the same as its own sensitive information, but never less than reasonable). The changed text, "Recipient will treat all CI with the degree of care that Recipient wants," gives the Recipient complete discretion. The Contradicted Text states "Recipient will treat all CI with the degree of care that Recipient wants." This creates a direct contradiction because the original ensures a baseline protection, while the altered text could allow for negligent handling of CI if the recipient doesn't value the CI, because 'the degree of care that Recipient wants' could be interpreted as no care at all, contradicting the intent of a non-disclosure agreement and the other confidentiality obligations in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will stop use of tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient will never be required to return or destroy the CI.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Recipient to stop using and return/destroy tangible CI upon request, unless legally prohibited. The new clause adds that the Recipient will never be required to return or destroy the CI which directly contradicts the previous statement that they will return or destroy the CI upon request. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the Recipient's obligations concerning the return or destruction of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with the degree of care that Recipient wants. Recipient will stop use of tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient will never be required to return or destroy the CI. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the contradicted text. The original text states the 'Recipient will stop use of tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law', which means they might need to return or destroy the CI. The changed text adds, 'Recipient will never be required to return or destroy the CI' which directly conflicts with the original statement and the contradicted text as a whole. The contradiction is clear, direct, and impactful, creating ambiguity about the Recipient's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI. However, Recipient may use the CI to develop their own products and services, and Discloser grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use any patents or intellectual property contained within the CI for that purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original states that the Recipient receives no license or IP rights to the CI. The added clause gives Recipient a non-exclusive license. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section. It is unclear whether the Recipient is permitted to use the intellectual property contained within the CI or not. This directly contradicts the first part of the sentence, giving rise to an in-text contradiction within Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Other Rights. All Discloser CI remains the property of Dis closer. Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI. However, Recipient may use the CI to develop their own products and services, and Discloser grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use any patents or intellectual property contained within the CI for that purpose. Each party agrees that Recipient (A) may independently develop and acquire software, hardware, and other technology fo r its own products, services, and processes, and that having access to Discloser’s CI does not prohibit Recipient from freely developing or acquiring similar products without obligation to Discloser; and (B) is free to use the residuals of CI for any purpo se, where “residuals” means that CI disclosed in non- tangible form that may be retained in the memories of representatives of Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI.' The changed text adds, 'However, Recipient may use the CI to develop their own products and services, and Discloser grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use any patents or intellectual property contained within the CI for that purpose.' This directly contradicts the initial statement by granting a non-exclusive license, creating a clear in-text contradiction within the contradicted text. The contradiction lies in the conflicting assertions that the Recipient both does and does not have intellectual property rights or a license to the CI.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days ’ written notice . However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days’ written notice. However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA, but only until the termination date, after which the Recipient has no further obligations regarding the CI.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the obligations for CI provided will remain under the terms of this NDA. The new clause states that after the termination date the Recipient has no further obligations. This introduces a contradiction, is the CI provided under the NDA still subject to the NDA after the agreement is terminated, or are the conditions no longer valid? If the terms were to be still valid, then the last part of the edited clause is not valid, and vice versa. This contradicts the first half of the sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination . Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days’ written notice. However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA, but only until the termination date, after which the Recipient has no further obligations regarding the CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding the confidentiality obligations after termination. The original text states all CI remains subject to the NDA terms, while the changed text limits this obligation until the termination date. The contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text in this case) creates an in-text contradiction because it implies both that the CI remains subject to the NDA terms indefinitely AND that it does not after the termination date. Thus it's a structural flaw, where the same clause has conflicting implications depending on how it's read.
